fixture.description: An access-control predicate returns the negation of the role membership check, inverting the decision: it grants access to users who LACK the required role and denies those who have it.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that canAccess returns the NEGATION of the role check (`!user.roles.includes(required)`), inverting the intended decision documented in the comment — it grants access to users who do NOT have the required role and denies those who do (an authorization bypass / inverted condition). It should recommend removing the `!`. Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the inverted access logic is not surfaced.

expected_behavior: Bugs & Security agent flags the inverted boolean (authorization bypass)
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT treat this as a stylistic preference
Does NOT require external call sites to confirm the inversion
